item.title: DBSegFormer: A Dual-Branch Framework based on Multi-scale Information Fusion for Semantic Segmentation
item.dc:creator: Fenger Li
He Yan
Maocheng Zhao
Liang Qi
Liyong Fu
Qiaolin Ye
item.prism:publicationName: Information Fusion
item.description: This study introduced DBSegFormer, an efficient multi-scale fusion framework for semantic segmentation. It features a dual-branch Transformer architecture, where the main branch uses SegFormer’s MixVision Transformer for global semantic learning, and a convolutional branch captures fine-grained details like textures and edges. The Multi-Scale Attention Fusion (MSAF) module combines content- and scale-adaptive features using dilated convolutions and gated attention, improving detail preservation. The fused features are processed by a lightweight MLP decoder, avoiding complex up-sampling and enhancing efficiency. Experiments demonstrate significant performance improvements over CNN/Transformer baselines, with efficient memory and latency usage, and strong zero-shot generalization across distribution shifts, making DBSegFormer a promising approach for high-precision, resource-efficient segmentation. Semantic segmentation assigns a semantic label to every pixel and is crucial for detailed scene understanding. The core challenge is to capture long-range global context while preserving high-resolution local details needed for precise boundaries, without relying on heavy decoders. We propose DBSegFormer, an efficient framework-based on multi-scale information fusion that combines a dual-branch Transformer backbone with a lightweight multilayer perceptron (MLP) decoder. The design embodies two key innovations: (i) a dual-branch architecture in which the main branch employs SegFormer’s MixVision Transformer to learn multi-scale global semantics, while a lightweight convolutional branch focuses on fine-grained details (e.g., textures, edges, small objects) often degraded by deep down-sampling; and (ii) a Multi-Scale Attention Fusion (MSAF) module that performs content- and scale-adaptive integration via multi-scale dilated convolutions and gated attention, yielding complementary, detail-aware representations. The fused features are then aggregated by a minimal MLP head, avoiding complex up-sampling heads and improving efficiency and stability. Extensive experiments across multiple public benchmarks show consistent gains over strong CNN/Transformer baselines while maintaining a favorable trade-off between segmentation accuracy and computational cost. These results indicate that decoupling semantic abstraction from detail retention and reunifying them through adaptive multi-scale fusion provides a simple yet effective solution for high-precision semantic segmentation. Published: 2026-05-07T16:08:37+00:00 Venue: Information Fusion Score: 0.841 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Fenger Li; He Yan; Maocheng Zhao; Liang Qi; Liyong Fu; Qiaolin Ye

Journal: Information Fusion

DOI: 10.1016/j.inffus.2026.104454

Relevance: 0.841 (must_read)

This study introduced DBSegFormer, an efficient multi-scale fusion framework for semantic segmentation. It features a dual-branch Transformer architecture, where the main branch uses SegFormer’s MixVision Transformer for global semantic learning, and a convolutional branch captures fine-grained details like textures and edges. The Multi-Scale Attention Fusion (MSAF) module combines content- and scale-adaptive features using dilated convolutions and gated attention, improving detail preservation. The fused features are processed by a lightweight MLP decoder, avoiding complex up-sampling and enhancing efficiency. Experiments demonstrate significant performance improvements over CNN/Transformer baselines, with efficient memory and latency usage, and strong zero-shot generalization across distribution shifts, making DBSegFormer a promising approach for high-precision, resource-efficient segmentation. Semantic segmentation assigns a semantic label to every pixel and is crucial for detailed scene understanding. The core challenge is to capture long-range global context while preserving high-resolution local details needed for precise boundaries, without relying on heavy decoders. We propose DBSegFormer, an efficient framework-based on multi-scale information fusion that combines a dual-branch Transformer backbone with a lightweight multilayer perceptron (MLP) decoder. The design embodies two key innovations: (i) a dual-branch architecture in which the main branch employs SegFormer’s MixVision Transformer to learn multi-scale global semantics, while a lightweight convolutional branch focuses on fine-grained details (e.g., textures, edges, small objects) often degraded by deep down-sampling; and (ii) a Multi-Scale Attention Fusion (MSAF) module that performs content- and scale-adaptive integration via multi-scale dilated convolutions and gated attention, yielding complementary, detail-aware representations. The fused features are then aggregated by a minimal MLP head, avoiding complex up-sampling heads and improving efficiency and stability. Extensive experiments across multiple public benchmarks show consistent gains over strong CNN/Transformer baselines while maintaining a favorable trade-off between segmentation accuracy and computational cost. These results indicate that decoupling semantic abstraction from detail retention and reunifying them through adaptive multi-scale fusion provides a simple yet effective solution for high-precision semantic segmentation.

item.title: DASFA-Net for Accurate Ship Detection in Optical Remote Sensing Images
item.dc:creator: Hanfu Li
Tianwei Zhang
Shikai Jiang
Xiyang Zhi
Dawei Wang
Lianru Gao
Dong Yang
Jindong Li
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
item.description: Ship detection in optical remote sensing imagery is pivotal for various civilian and military applications. However, due to the presence of cluttered backgrounds and the significant variations in ship scales, accurately detecting ships in complex maritime environments remains challenging. To address these challenges, we propose the Dual Attention and Scale-aware Feature Alignment Network (DASFA-Net), to effectively detect multi-scale ship targets under clutter interference. The DASFA-Net comprises two primary components: Deformable Spatial Attention Module with Channel Integration (DSAM-CI) and Bidirectional Flow Alignment Network (BiFAN). The DSAM-CI enhances discrimination between ships and distractors by jointly modeling correlations across the channel and spatial domains and applying deformable spatial attention. Concurrently, BiFAN employs a bidirectional feature flow alignment strategy to resolve spatial misalignment in feature fusion, significantly improving the quality of multi-scale feature fusion. Lastly, we design the Dynamic Alpha Complete Intersection over Union (DA-CIoU) based on prior knowledge of target characteristics to explicitly refine our detector for maritime ships. Extensive experiments on three challenging ship detection benchmarks, including HRSC2016, SCCOS, and FGSRCS have demonstrated the superiority of our DASFA. Published: 2026-05-08T19:39:47+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing Score: 0.836 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Hanfu Li; Tianwei Zhang; Shikai Jiang; Xiyang Zhi; Dawei Wang; Lianru Gao; Dong Yang; Jindong Li

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/tgrs.2026.3691594

Relevance: 0.836 (must_read)

Ship detection in optical remote sensing imagery is pivotal for various civilian and military applications. However, due to the presence of cluttered backgrounds and the significant variations in ship scales, accurately detecting ships in complex maritime environments remains challenging. To address these challenges, we propose the Dual Attention and Scale-aware Feature Alignment Network (DASFA-Net), to effectively detect multi-scale ship targets under clutter interference. The DASFA-Net comprises two primary components: Deformable Spatial Attention Module with Channel Integration (DSAM-CI) and Bidirectional Flow Alignment Network (BiFAN). The DSAM-CI enhances discrimination between ships and distractors by jointly modeling correlations across the channel and spatial domains and applying deformable spatial attention. Concurrently, BiFAN employs a bidirectional feature flow alignment strategy to resolve spatial misalignment in feature fusion, significantly improving the quality of multi-scale feature fusion. Lastly, we design the Dynamic Alpha Complete Intersection over Union (DA-CIoU) based on prior knowledge of target characteristics to explicitly refine our detector for maritime ships. Extensive experiments on three challenging ship detection benchmarks, including HRSC2016, SCCOS, and FGSRCS have demonstrated the superiority of our DASFA.

item.title: Bidirectional Motion Perception Transformer Network for Multi-frame Infrared Small Target Detection
item.dc:creator: Zhiwei Jiang
Xiumei Wang
Xiangyang Li
Chunna Tian
Heng Zhou
Zhenxi Zhang
Shiguo Chen
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
item.description: Multi-frame infrared small target detection (MIRSTD) aims to detect weak and fast-moving targets by leveraging temporal information across consecutive frames. However, existing MIRSTD methods primarily focus on intra-frame spatial contrast while insufficiently modeling temporal dependencies, leading to weak motion perception and poor temporal coherence. To address these issues, we propose BMPTransNet, a Bidirectional Motion Perception Transformer Network that effectively captures motion information across consecutive frames. First, a multi-scale encoder with a Spatial-embedded Channel Interaction Block (SCIB) is employed to generate fused spatial features, which are redistributed through a pyramid structure to improve the semantic distinction. Second, a Bidirectional Motion Perception Branch (BMPB) is introduced to align multi-frame features, extracting salient temporal cues while suppressing dynamic background interference. Third, a multi-frame joint iterative decoder augments initial queries with additional motion-guided queries derived from shallow features and temporal motion information. Finally, a self-supervised motion-consistency learning strategy guides the network to maintain temporal coherence and spatial accuracy across consecutive frames. Experimental results on three public datasets demonstrate that BMPTransNet achieves accurate detection of moving infrared small targets. Published: 2026-05-08T19:42:07+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology Score: 0.835 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Zhiwei Jiang; Xiumei Wang; Xiangyang Li; Chunna Tian; Heng Zhou; Zhenxi Zhang; Shiguo Chen

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology

DOI: 10.1109/tcsvt.2026.3691319

Relevance: 0.835 (must_read)

Multi-frame infrared small target detection (MIRSTD) aims to detect weak and fast-moving targets by leveraging temporal information across consecutive frames. However, existing MIRSTD methods primarily focus on intra-frame spatial contrast while insufficiently modeling temporal dependencies, leading to weak motion perception and poor temporal coherence. To address these issues, we propose BMPTransNet, a Bidirectional Motion Perception Transformer Network that effectively captures motion information across consecutive frames. First, a multi-scale encoder with a Spatial-embedded Channel Interaction Block (SCIB) is employed to generate fused spatial features, which are redistributed through a pyramid structure to improve the semantic distinction. Second, a Bidirectional Motion Perception Branch (BMPB) is introduced to align multi-frame features, extracting salient temporal cues while suppressing dynamic background interference. Third, a multi-frame joint iterative decoder augments initial queries with additional motion-guided queries derived from shallow features and temporal motion information. Finally, a self-supervised motion-consistency learning strategy guides the network to maintain temporal coherence and spatial accuracy across consecutive frames. Experimental results on three public datasets demonstrate that BMPTransNet achieves accurate detection of moving infrared small targets.

item.title: Generative Learning Imaging Framework for Millimeter Wave Synthetic Aperture Radar
item.dc:creator: Mou Wang
Yifei Hu
Hao Zhang
Xiang Cai
Jiahui Chen
Shunjun Wei
Jun Shi
Guolong Cui
Lingjiang Kong
Yongxin Guo
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
item.description: Accurately reconstructing synthetic aperture radar (SAR) images from incomplete echo measurements is the fundamental problem in simplifying SAR systems and reducing sensing costs. In solving this problem, the recently emerged deep learning SAR imaging concept shows high potential in offering both high accuracy and efficiency. However, such approaches face challenges in network topology designing, weights initialization, and dataset construction, which hinder their practical deployments. To address these problems, we propose a generative learning imaging framework, namely, generative learning imaging framework (GLIm), for millimeter wave (mmWave) SAR in tasks of sparsely sensing scenarios. In our scheme, the targeting images are generated from a specifically designed SAR image generator by mapping low-dimensional random noise to the scattering domain. In addition, the SAR image generator is trained based on a compound loss function where the incomplete echo measurements serve as the supervised signal. Unlike traditional algorithms, the algorithm proposed in this article solves the optimal solution purely through numerical propagation by online learning, avoiding the theoretical derivation of the iterative optimization process. Finally, experiments are carried out on both simulated and real-measured data, and both numerical and visual results demonstrate the viability of the proposed GLIm in the tasks of reconstructing mmWave SAR images from sparsely sampled echo measurements. Published: 2026-05-08T19:40:39+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems Score: 0.829 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Mou Wang; Yifei Hu; Hao Zhang; Xiang Cai; Jiahui Chen; Shunjun Wei; Jun Shi; Guolong Cui; Lingjiang Kong; Yongxin Guo

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems

DOI: 10.1109/tnnls.2026.3686777

Relevance: 0.829 (must_read)

Accurately reconstructing synthetic aperture radar (SAR) images from incomplete echo measurements is the fundamental problem in simplifying SAR systems and reducing sensing costs. In solving this problem, the recently emerged deep learning SAR imaging concept shows high potential in offering both high accuracy and efficiency. However, such approaches face challenges in network topology designing, weights initialization, and dataset construction, which hinder their practical deployments. To address these problems, we propose a generative learning imaging framework, namely, generative learning imaging framework (GLIm), for millimeter wave (mmWave) SAR in tasks of sparsely sensing scenarios. In our scheme, the targeting images are generated from a specifically designed SAR image generator by mapping low-dimensional random noise to the scattering domain. In addition, the SAR image generator is trained based on a compound loss function where the incomplete echo measurements serve as the supervised signal. Unlike traditional algorithms, the algorithm proposed in this article solves the optimal solution purely through numerical propagation by online learning, avoiding the theoretical derivation of the iterative optimization process. Finally, experiments are carried out on both simulated and real-measured data, and both numerical and visual results demonstrate the viability of the proposed GLIm in the tasks of reconstructing mmWave SAR images from sparsely sampled echo measurements.

item.title: Cross-domain Few-shot Object Detection with Multi-modal Textual Enrichment
item.dc:creator: Zeyu Shangguan
Daniel Seita
Mohammad Rostami
item.prism:publicationName: International Journal of Computer Vision
item.description: Advancements in cross-modal feature extraction and integration have significantly enhanced performance in few-shot learning tasks. However, current multi-modal object detection (MM-OD) methods often experience notable performance degradation when encountering substantial domain shifts. We propose that incorporating rich textual information can enable the model to establish a more robust knowledge relationship between visual instances and their corresponding language descriptions, thereby mitigating the challenges of domain shift. Specifically, we focus on the problem of Cross-Domain Multi-Modal Few-Shot Object Detection (CDMM-FSOD) and introduce a meta-learning-based framework designed to leverage rich textual semantics as an auxiliary modality to achieve effective domain adaptation. Our new architecture incorporates two key components: (i) A multi-modal feature aggregation module, which aligns visual and linguistic feature embeddings to ensure cohesive integration across modalities. (ii) A rich text semantic rectification module, which employs bidirectional text feature generation to refine multi-modal feature alignment, thereby enhancing understanding of language and its application in object detection. We evaluate the proposed method on three cross-domain object detection benchmarks and demonstrate that it significantly surpasses existing few-shot object detection approaches. The implementation of our method is publicly accessible at: https://github.com/zshanggu/CDMM. . Published: 2026-05-07T19:16:13+00:00 Venue: International Journal of Computer Vision Score: 0.823 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Zeyu Shangguan; Daniel Seita; Mohammad Rostami

Journal: International Journal of Computer Vision

DOI: 10.1007/s11263-026-02839-7

Relevance: 0.823 (must_read)

Advancements in cross-modal feature extraction and integration have significantly enhanced performance in few-shot learning tasks. However, current multi-modal object detection (MM-OD) methods often experience notable performance degradation when encountering substantial domain shifts. We propose that incorporating rich textual information can enable the model to establish a more robust knowledge relationship between visual instances and their corresponding language descriptions, thereby mitigating the challenges of domain shift. Specifically, we focus on the problem of Cross-Domain Multi-Modal Few-Shot Object Detection (CDMM-FSOD) and introduce a meta-learning-based framework designed to leverage rich textual semantics as an auxiliary modality to achieve effective domain adaptation. Our new architecture incorporates two key components: (i) A multi-modal feature aggregation module, which aligns visual and linguistic feature embeddings to ensure cohesive integration across modalities. (ii) A rich text semantic rectification module, which employs bidirectional text feature generation to refine multi-modal feature alignment, thereby enhancing understanding of language and its application in object detection. We evaluate the proposed method on three cross-domain object detection benchmarks and demonstrate that it significantly surpasses existing few-shot object detection approaches. The implementation of our method is publicly accessible at: https://github.com/zshanggu/CDMM. .

item.title: D <sup>2</sup> FT-Net: Frequency-Spatial Dual Domain Fine-Tuning of Vision Foundation Models for Remote Sensing Domain Generalization Semantic Segmentation
item.dc:creator: Zimeng Shen
Jue Wang
Tianyu Wei
Wenchao Liu
Xiaoyan Lu
Liang Chen
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
item.description: In recent years, remote sensing domain generalization semantic segmentation has attracted increasing attention due to significant domain shifts caused by variations in sensors, imaging conditions, and geographic regions. Vision foundation models (VFMs) possess strong general-purpose feature extraction capabilities and can be effectively transferred across diverse data, showing great potential for remote sensing domain generalization semantic segmentation. However, existing VFM-based spatial domain parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods struggle to handle pronounced cross-domain intra-class variations in remote sensing. To address this issue, the Adaptive Frequency Aware Adapter (AFA-Adapter) is proposed, which adaptively selects frequency components to improve cross-domain intra-class feature consistency. Building upon this, the Spatial Multi-Prototype Adapter (SMP-Adapter) is proposed, which clusters multiple prototypes for features of each land-cover category to model complex intra-class diversity. Land-cover features are then weighted by their nearest intra-class prototype, thereby enhancing the discriminability of easily confused features at feature cluster boundaries. By integrating these two modules, we propose a Frequency–Spatial Dual Domain Fine-Tuning Network (D2FT-Net), which effectively alleviates cross-domain intra-class variations and improves the generalization capability of VFMs for remote sensing domain generalization semantic segmentation. Extensive experiments under four cross-domain settings demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed D2FT-Net, which achieves an average mIoU improvement of 1.09% over state-of-the-art methods, with the best gain reaching 1.64%. The source code will be released at https://github.com/ssshen0315/D2FT-Net. Published: 2026-05-07T19:53:06+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing Score: 0.822 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Zimeng Shen; Jue Wang; Tianyu Wei; Wenchao Liu; Xiaoyan Lu; Liang Chen

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/tgrs.2026.3691111

Relevance: 0.822 (must_read)

In recent years, remote sensing domain generalization semantic segmentation has attracted increasing attention due to significant domain shifts caused by variations in sensors, imaging conditions, and geographic regions. Vision foundation models (VFMs) possess strong general-purpose feature extraction capabilities and can be effectively transferred across diverse data, showing great potential for remote sensing domain generalization semantic segmentation. However, existing VFM-based spatial domain parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods struggle to handle pronounced cross-domain intra-class variations in remote sensing. To address this issue, the Adaptive Frequency Aware Adapter (AFA-Adapter) is proposed, which adaptively selects frequency components to improve cross-domain intra-class feature consistency. Building upon this, the Spatial Multi-Prototype Adapter (SMP-Adapter) is proposed, which clusters multiple prototypes for features of each land-cover category to model complex intra-class diversity. Land-cover features are then weighted by their nearest intra-class prototype, thereby enhancing the discriminability of easily confused features at feature cluster boundaries. By integrating these two modules, we propose a Frequency–Spatial Dual Domain Fine-Tuning Network (D2FT-Net), which effectively alleviates cross-domain intra-class variations and improves the generalization capability of VFMs for remote sensing domain generalization semantic segmentation. Extensive experiments under four cross-domain settings demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed D2FT-Net, which achieves an average mIoU improvement of 1.09% over state-of-the-art methods, with the best gain reaching 1.64%. The source code will be released at https://github.com/ssshen0315/D2FT-Net.

item.title: A Comprehensive Survey of Pretrained Models for Autonomous Driving: Methods and Applications
item.dc:creator: Renze Hu
Zhishuai Yin
Linzhen Nie
Yinghan Zhou
Hongchen Guo
item.prism:publicationName: Information Fusion
item.description: Pretrained models (PTMs), developed under the pretraining-to-tuning paradigm, have significantly advanced the capabilities of autonomous driving (AD) systems, demonstrating improved generalization, robustness, and engineering efficiency compared with models trained from scratch. In this paper, we provide a comprehensive and comparative survey of PTMs applied to AD over the past five years. Through an extensive review of existing literature, we first provide a detailed overview of PTMs from the perspectives of key pretraining strategies and tuning methods. Then, we propose a task-oriented taxonomy encompassing both modular and end-to-end driving systems, elucidating how PTMs are leveraged to address the challenges encountered across diverse driving tasks. In addition, we summarize representative benchmark datasets, simulation platforms, and open-source implementations to support systematic and reproducible research within the community. Finally, we discuss the remaining challenges associated with applying PTMs to AD and outline promising directions for future exploration. To ensure the continued relevance and timeliness of this study, we maintain a dynamic repository of related literature and resources, which is publicly accessible at PTM4AD . Published: 2026-05-08T12:53:52+00:00 Venue: Information Fusion Score: 0.818 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Renze Hu; Zhishuai Yin; Linzhen Nie; Yinghan Zhou; Hongchen Guo

Journal: Information Fusion

DOI: 10.1016/j.inffus.2026.104457

Relevance: 0.818 (must_read)

Pretrained models (PTMs), developed under the pretraining-to-tuning paradigm, have significantly advanced the capabilities of autonomous driving (AD) systems, demonstrating improved generalization, robustness, and engineering efficiency compared with models trained from scratch. In this paper, we provide a comprehensive and comparative survey of PTMs applied to AD over the past five years. Through an extensive review of existing literature, we first provide a detailed overview of PTMs from the perspectives of key pretraining strategies and tuning methods. Then, we propose a task-oriented taxonomy encompassing both modular and end-to-end driving systems, elucidating how PTMs are leveraged to address the challenges encountered across diverse driving tasks. In addition, we summarize representative benchmark datasets, simulation platforms, and open-source implementations to support systematic and reproducible research within the community. Finally, we discuss the remaining challenges associated with applying PTMs to AD and outline promising directions for future exploration. To ensure the continued relevance and timeliness of this study, we maintain a dynamic repository of related literature and resources, which is publicly accessible at PTM4AD .

item.title: EchoSR: Efficient Context Harnessing for Lightweight Image Super-Resolution
item.dc:creator: Hanli Zhao
Binhao Wang
Shihao Zhao
Tao Wang
Kaihao Zhang
Wanglong Lu
item.prism:publicationName: Information Fusion
item.description: Image super-resolution (SR) aims to reconstruct high-quality, high-resolution (HR) images from low-resolution (LR) inputs and plays a critical role in various downstream applications. Despite recent advancements, balancing reconstruction fidelity and computational efficiency remains a fundamental challenge, particularly in resource-constrained scenarios. While existing lightweight methods attempt to expand receptive fields, many of them either incur substantial computational overhead, naively scale up kernel sizes, or lack mechanisms for coherent multi-scale integration, limiting their overall effectiveness and scalability. To address these limitations, we propose EchoSR, an efficient context-harnessing framework for lightweight image super-resolution, which unifies multi-scale receptive field modeling and hierarchical context fusion. EchoSR decouples feature learning into disentangled local, multi-scale, and global modeling stages through an efficient context-harnessing strategy, and further promotes seamless cross-scale integration via a cross-scale overlapping fusion mechanism. Extensive experiments have shown that EchoSR consistently outperforms state-of-the-art lightweight super-resolution methods across multiple benchmarks, while also achieving a faster speed ( ∼ 2 × ). The source code is available at https://github.com/funnyWang-Echoes/EchoSR . Published: 2026-05-08T23:37:14+00:00 Venue: Information Fusion Score: 0.811 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Hanli Zhao; Binhao Wang; Shihao Zhao; Tao Wang; Kaihao Zhang; Wanglong Lu

Journal: Information Fusion

DOI: 10.1016/j.inffus.2026.104471

Relevance: 0.811 (must_read)

Image super-resolution (SR) aims to reconstruct high-quality, high-resolution (HR) images from low-resolution (LR) inputs and plays a critical role in various downstream applications. Despite recent advancements, balancing reconstruction fidelity and computational efficiency remains a fundamental challenge, particularly in resource-constrained scenarios. While existing lightweight methods attempt to expand receptive fields, many of them either incur substantial computational overhead, naively scale up kernel sizes, or lack mechanisms for coherent multi-scale integration, limiting their overall effectiveness and scalability. To address these limitations, we propose EchoSR, an efficient context-harnessing framework for lightweight image super-resolution, which unifies multi-scale receptive field modeling and hierarchical context fusion. EchoSR decouples feature learning into disentangled local, multi-scale, and global modeling stages through an efficient context-harnessing strategy, and further promotes seamless cross-scale integration via a cross-scale overlapping fusion mechanism. Extensive experiments have shown that EchoSR consistently outperforms state-of-the-art lightweight super-resolution methods across multiple benchmarks, while also achieving a faster speed ( ∼ 2 × ). The source code is available at https://github.com/funnyWang-Echoes/EchoSR .

item.title: Point Cloud Semantic Segmentation via Transformer-Mamba Fusion: A Hierarchical Approach for Collapsed Building Detection
item.dc:creator: Jiayi Li
Rui Tian
Zonghan Shang
item.prism:publicationName: Information Fusion
item.description: After natural disasters, rapidly and accurately detecting collapsed buildings is crucial for emergency rescue, post-disaster assessment, and reconstruction. However, existing point cloud-based detection methods for buildings have notable limitations: convolutional neural networks struggle to capture global structural relationships, Transformers exhibit quadratic computational complexity when processing large-scale point clouds, and Mamba lacks explicit global attention mechanisms. To address these issues, this paper proposes the CollTransMam network for collapsed building detection, which employs a hierarchical encoder-decoder architecture that innovatively combines the global attention advantage of Transformer with the linear complexity long-sequence modeling capability of Mamba. Specifically, the Fusion Conv module generates compact feature representations through efficient sampling, clustering, and feature fusion; the Grouping and Aggregation Block (GAB) module captures spatial correlations through multi-scale grouping and aggregation; the 3D Zigzag path converts unordered point clouds into ordered sequences; and the final Transformer-Mamba Fusion (TM-Fusion) module integrates the strengths of both Transformer and Mamba to achieve efficient global-local feature modeling. Experiments on two real earthquake disaster LiDAR point cloud datasets, KECB-PCD and PAPCB-PCD, show that the CollTransMam network significantly outperforms mainstream CNN, Transformer, and Mamba-based methods in core metrics such as accuracy, recall, IoU, OA, and mIoU. At the same time, the network has only 5.3M parameters and 5.4 FLOPs, with a computational complexity much lower than similar models, achieving a good balance between detection accuracy and efficiency, providing a reliable technical solution for rapid and accurate detection of collapsed buildings after disasters. Published: 2026-05-07T07:04:26+00:00 Venue: Information Fusion Score: 0.810 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Jiayi Li; Rui Tian; Zonghan Shang

Journal: Information Fusion

DOI: 10.1016/j.inffus.2026.104450

Relevance: 0.810 (must_read)

After natural disasters, rapidly and accurately detecting collapsed buildings is crucial for emergency rescue, post-disaster assessment, and reconstruction. However, existing point cloud-based detection methods for buildings have notable limitations: convolutional neural networks struggle to capture global structural relationships, Transformers exhibit quadratic computational complexity when processing large-scale point clouds, and Mamba lacks explicit global attention mechanisms. To address these issues, this paper proposes the CollTransMam network for collapsed building detection, which employs a hierarchical encoder-decoder architecture that innovatively combines the global attention advantage of Transformer with the linear complexity long-sequence modeling capability of Mamba. Specifically, the Fusion Conv module generates compact feature representations through efficient sampling, clustering, and feature fusion; the Grouping and Aggregation Block (GAB) module captures spatial correlations through multi-scale grouping and aggregation; the 3D Zigzag path converts unordered point clouds into ordered sequences; and the final Transformer-Mamba Fusion (TM-Fusion) module integrates the strengths of both Transformer and Mamba to achieve efficient global-local feature modeling. Experiments on two real earthquake disaster LiDAR point cloud datasets, KECB-PCD and PAPCB-PCD, show that the CollTransMam network significantly outperforms mainstream CNN, Transformer, and Mamba-based methods in core metrics such as accuracy, recall, IoU, OA, and mIoU. At the same time, the network has only 5.3M parameters and 5.4 FLOPs, with a computational complexity much lower than similar models, achieving a good balance between detection accuracy and efficiency, providing a reliable technical solution for rapid and accurate detection of collapsed buildings after disasters.

item.title: A Fine-Grained Semantic-Driven Pedestrian Identity-Aware Method for Visible and Infrared Image Fusion
item.dc:creator: Xiaowen Liu
Hongtao Huo
Haozhe Cao
Renhua Wang
Xu Dong
Shan Pang
Jing Li
item.prism:publicationName: Information Fusion
item.description: Visible and infrared image fusion (VIF) aims to integrate multi-modal complementary information for comprehensive scene representation, but existing vision-guided and semantic-driven VIF methods rely on coarse-grained consistency constraints. This causes modal shift and fine-grained semantic loss, severely degrading the performance of fused images in high-precision downstream tasks such as person re-identification (ReID), which requires accurate pedestrian identity features. To address this issue, we propose a unified optimization framework that integrates pedestrian identity perception and VIF, with fine-grained semantic constraints to learn a more discriminative cross-modal fusion space. First, we design a simulated pedestrian-scene synthesis method to generate training data with rich backgrounds and identity-annotated foregrounds by fusing mainstream ReID and VIF datasets. Second, we present DPSFusion, a fusion method based on a dual-stage progressive semantic refinement mechanism, which includes Feature-level Coarse Optimization (FCO) for initial cross-modal semantic alignment and Semantic-level Fine-tuning (SFT) for precise semantic refinement via cross-task supervision. Additionally, we construct the first identity-annotated VIF validation dataset to directly quantify the retention of identity-related fine-grained information in fusion results. Qualitative and quantitative experiments confirm that our method outperforms state-of-the-art (SOTA) approaches in visual presentation, and validation on object detection and ReID tasks verifies its superior ability to preserve both coarse-grained and fine-grained semantics. The code and data will be publicly released upon paper publication. Published: 2026-05-08T23:37:25+00:00 Venue: Information Fusion Score: 0.809 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Xiaowen Liu; Hongtao Huo; Haozhe Cao; Renhua Wang; Xu Dong; Shan Pang; Jing Li

Journal: Information Fusion

DOI: 10.1016/j.inffus.2026.104453

Relevance: 0.809 (must_read)

Visible and infrared image fusion (VIF) aims to integrate multi-modal complementary information for comprehensive scene representation, but existing vision-guided and semantic-driven VIF methods rely on coarse-grained consistency constraints. This causes modal shift and fine-grained semantic loss, severely degrading the performance of fused images in high-precision downstream tasks such as person re-identification (ReID), which requires accurate pedestrian identity features. To address this issue, we propose a unified optimization framework that integrates pedestrian identity perception and VIF, with fine-grained semantic constraints to learn a more discriminative cross-modal fusion space. First, we design a simulated pedestrian-scene synthesis method to generate training data with rich backgrounds and identity-annotated foregrounds by fusing mainstream ReID and VIF datasets. Second, we present DPSFusion, a fusion method based on a dual-stage progressive semantic refinement mechanism, which includes Feature-level Coarse Optimization (FCO) for initial cross-modal semantic alignment and Semantic-level Fine-tuning (SFT) for precise semantic refinement via cross-task supervision. Additionally, we construct the first identity-annotated VIF validation dataset to directly quantify the retention of identity-related fine-grained information in fusion results. Qualitative and quantitative experiments confirm that our method outperforms state-of-the-art (SOTA) approaches in visual presentation, and validation on object detection and ReID tasks verifies its superior ability to preserve both coarse-grained and fine-grained semantics. The code and data will be publicly released upon paper publication.

item.title: Adaptive 3D Convolution for Remote Sensing Image Fusion
item.dc:creator: Siran Peng
Xiangyu Zhu
Shang-Qi Deng
Liang-Jian Deng
Zhen Lei
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
item.description: Remote sensing image fusion aims to create a high-resolution multi/hyper-spectral image from a high-resolution image with limited spectral information and a low-resolution image with abundant spectral data. Recently, deep learning (DL) techniques have shown significant effectiveness in this area. Most DL-based methods approach image fusion as a 2D problem by encoding spectral information into feature map channels. However, our research suggests that this strategy introduces notable spectral distortions. In contrast, some methods consider spectral data as an additional dimension, utilizing standard 3D convolutions to preserve spectral information. Nevertheless, in a standard 3D convolutional layer, the same set of kernels is applied across all input regions, which we have found to be sub-optimal for image fusion. Furthermore, standard 3D convolutions necessitate substantial computational resources. To address these challenges, we propose a novel convolutional paradigm called Adaptive 3D Convolution (Ada3D) for remote sensing image fusion. Ada3D applies a unique set of 3D kernels to each input voxel, enabling the capture of fine-grained details. These adaptive kernels are generated through a two-step process: (i) spatial and spectral kernels are derived from their respective image sources; (ii) these two types of kernels are then combined to form content-aware 3D kernels that effectively integrate spatial and spectral information. Additionally, adaptive biases are introduced to enhance the convolutional outcome at the voxel level. Furthermore, we incorporate the group convolution technique to reduce computational complexity. As a result, Ada3D offers full adaptivity in an efficient manner. Evaluation results across five datasets demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance, underscoring the superiority of Ada3D. The code is available at https://github.com/PSRben/Ada3D. Published: 2026-05-08T19:43:05+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing Score: 0.807 (must_read)

Authors: Siran Peng; Xiangyu Zhu; Shang-Qi Deng; Liang-Jian Deng; Zhen Lei

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing

DOI: 10.1109/tip.2026.3689418

Relevance: 0.807 (must_read)

Remote sensing image fusion aims to create a high-resolution multi/hyper-spectral image from a high-resolution image with limited spectral information and a low-resolution image with abundant spectral data. Recently, deep learning (DL) techniques have shown significant effectiveness in this area. Most DL-based methods approach image fusion as a 2D problem by encoding spectral information into feature map channels. However, our research suggests that this strategy introduces notable spectral distortions. In contrast, some methods consider spectral data as an additional dimension, utilizing standard 3D convolutions to preserve spectral information. Nevertheless, in a standard 3D convolutional layer, the same set of kernels is applied across all input regions, which we have found to be sub-optimal for image fusion. Furthermore, standard 3D convolutions necessitate substantial computational resources. To address these challenges, we propose a novel convolutional paradigm called Adaptive 3D Convolution (Ada3D) for remote sensing image fusion. Ada3D applies a unique set of 3D kernels to each input voxel, enabling the capture of fine-grained details. These adaptive kernels are generated through a two-step process: (i) spatial and spectral kernels are derived from their respective image sources; (ii) these two types of kernels are then combined to form content-aware 3D kernels that effectively integrate spatial and spectral information. Additionally, adaptive biases are introduced to enhance the convolutional outcome at the voxel level. Furthermore, we incorporate the group convolution technique to reduce computational complexity. As a result, Ada3D offers full adaptivity in an efficient manner. Evaluation results across five datasets demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance, underscoring the superiority of Ada3D. The code is available at https://github.com/PSRben/Ada3D.

item.title: D-PolTransGAN: Scattering Guided Hybrid Transformer-GAN for SAR-Optical Image Translation using Dual Polarization SAR
item.dc:creator: Jagrati Talreja
Tewodros Syum Gebre
Leila Hashemi-Beni
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
item.description: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) provides reliable imaging in all weather and lighting conditions, but its speckled appearance makes visual interpretation difficult and limits its integration with optical-based analyses. To address this challenge, we propose a new SAR-to-optical image translation framework tailored for flood assessment. The proposed model processes SAR VV and VH polarizations through separate feature extraction streams, enabling each stem to learn its unique scattering characteristics. These features are then fused using cross-attention to capture complementary information from both polarizations. The architecture combines convolutional networks for local detail extraction with transformer modules to model global context, all within a generative adversarial learning framework. We train and evaluate the model using the DEEPFLOOD, SEN1FLOODS11 and SEN12MS datasets, which provides well-aligned Sentinel-1 (VV, VH) and Sentinel-2 image pairs. Experimental results show that the proposed method outperforms existing approaches in terms of PSNR, SSIM, and perceptual quality (LPIPS), while preserving fine spatial details critical for flood analysis. Qualitative results on real flood events further demonstrate that the generated optical-like images recover visual structures and contextual information that are difficult to interpret in raw SAR imagery. https://github.com/JagratiTalreja01/D-PolTransGAN Published: 2026-05-08T19:40:24+00:00 Venue: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing Score: 0.806 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Jagrati Talreja; Tewodros Syum Gebre; Leila Hashemi-Beni

Journal: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/jstars.2026.3691677

Relevance: 0.806 (must_read)

Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) provides reliable imaging in all weather and lighting conditions, but its speckled appearance makes visual interpretation difficult and limits its integration with optical-based analyses. To address this challenge, we propose a new SAR-to-optical image translation framework tailored for flood assessment. The proposed model processes SAR VV and VH polarizations through separate feature extraction streams, enabling each stem to learn its unique scattering characteristics. These features are then fused using cross-attention to capture complementary information from both polarizations. The architecture combines convolutional networks for local detail extraction with transformer modules to model global context, all within a generative adversarial learning framework. We train and evaluate the model using the DEEPFLOOD, SEN1FLOODS11 and SEN12MS datasets, which provides well-aligned Sentinel-1 (VV, VH) and Sentinel-2 image pairs. Experimental results show that the proposed method outperforms existing approaches in terms of PSNR, SSIM, and perceptual quality (LPIPS), while preserving fine spatial details critical for flood analysis. Qualitative results on real flood events further demonstrate that the generated optical-like images recover visual structures and contextual information that are difficult to interpret in raw SAR imagery. https://github.com/JagratiTalreja01/D-PolTransGAN

item.title: Zero-Shot Neural Network Evaluation with Sample-Wise Activation Patterns
item.dc:creator: Yameng Peng
Andy Song
Haytham M. Fayek
Vic Ciesielski
Xiaojun Chang
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
item.description: Zero-shot proxies, also known as training-free metrics, are widely adopted to reduce the computational overhead in neural network evaluation for scenarios such as Neural Architecture Search (NAS), as they do not require any training. Existing zero-shot metrics have several limitations, including weak correlation with the true performance and poor generalisation across different networks or downstream tasks. For example, most of these metrics apply only to either convolutional neural networks (CNNs) or Transformers, but not both. To address these limitations, we propose Sample-Wise Activation Patterns (SWAP), and its derivative, SWAP-Score, a novel and highly effective zero-shot metric. SWAP-Score is broadly applicable across both architecture families and task domains, demonstrating strong predictive performance in the majority of tasks. This metric measures the expressivity of neural networks over a mini-batch of samples, showing a high correlation with the neural networks' ground-truth performance. For both CNNs and Transformers, the SWAP-Score outperforms existing zero-shot metrics across computer vision and natural language processing tasks. For instance, Spearman's correlation coefficient between the SWAP-Score and CIFAR-10 validation accuracy for DARTS CNNs is 0.93, and 0.71 for FlexiBERT Transformers on GLUE tasks. Moreover, SWAP-Score is label-independent, hence can be applied at the pre-training stage of language models to estimate their performance for downstream tasks. When applied to NAS, SWAP-empowered NAS, SWAP-NAS can achieve competitive performance using only approximately 6 and 9 minutes of GPU time, on CIFAR-10 and ImageNet respectively. Our code is available at: https://github.com/pym1024/SWAP_Universal. Published: 2026-05-07T19:53:00+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence Score: 0.805 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Yameng Peng; Andy Song; Haytham M. Fayek; Vic Ciesielski; Xiaojun Chang

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence

DOI: 10.1109/tpami.2026.3691075

Relevance: 0.805 (must_read)

Zero-shot proxies, also known as training-free metrics, are widely adopted to reduce the computational overhead in neural network evaluation for scenarios such as Neural Architecture Search (NAS), as they do not require any training. Existing zero-shot metrics have several limitations, including weak correlation with the true performance and poor generalisation across different networks or downstream tasks. For example, most of these metrics apply only to either convolutional neural networks (CNNs) or Transformers, but not both. To address these limitations, we propose Sample-Wise Activation Patterns (SWAP), and its derivative, SWAP-Score, a novel and highly effective zero-shot metric. SWAP-Score is broadly applicable across both architecture families and task domains, demonstrating strong predictive performance in the majority of tasks. This metric measures the expressivity of neural networks over a mini-batch of samples, showing a high correlation with the neural networks' ground-truth performance. For both CNNs and Transformers, the SWAP-Score outperforms existing zero-shot metrics across computer vision and natural language processing tasks. For instance, Spearman's correlation coefficient between the SWAP-Score and CIFAR-10 validation accuracy for DARTS CNNs is 0.93, and 0.71 for FlexiBERT Transformers on GLUE tasks. Moreover, SWAP-Score is label-independent, hence can be applied at the pre-training stage of language models to estimate their performance for downstream tasks. When applied to NAS, SWAP-empowered NAS, SWAP-NAS can achieve competitive performance using only approximately 6 and 9 minutes of GPU time, on CIFAR-10 and ImageNet respectively. Our code is available at: https://github.com/pym1024/SWAP_Universal.

item.title: Multimodal-Interactive GCN-Guided Space-Frequency Implicit Fusion Network for Semantic Segmentation of Optical and SAR Images
item.dc:creator: Bingxia Xin
Hongqing Li
Chunyu Zhu
Rongyuan Dai
Qiong Wu
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
item.description: Multi-source remote sensing images capture complementary information of ground objects and provide valuable inputs for semantic segmentation, and different sensors may produce modality inconsistencies and interference noise. Optical and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) segmentation is challenging for three main reasons. First, geometric distortions introduce scale inconsistencies. Second, speckle noise degrades signal quality. Third, subtle inter-class differences lead to misalignment between modalities. Collectively, these factors aggravate cross-modal contextual inconsistency and hinder accurate multimodal segmentation. We propose MSFNet, a graph-guided spatial-frequency implicit fusion network that unifies graph priors with continuous implicit representations. A dual-path encoder processes optical images with a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and SAR with a Dynamic Adaptive Graph Convolutional Network(DA-GCN) to capture topological/scattering structures. Fusion is performed by Graph-guided Spatial Implicit Sampling (GSIS) for geometry-aware alignment in the continuous coordinate space and Graph-guided Fourier Implicit Sampling(GFIS) for frequency-aware detail compensation and global context modulation. A multi-scale decoder yields fine-grained boundaries. On WHU-OPT-SAR, DFC23, YYX-OPT-SAR and DFC2025, MSFNet achieves state-of-the-art performance, with consistent gains in Boundary F1-score (Boundary-F1) and boundary intersection-over-union (bIoU), as well as robustness to misregistration and speckle. These results demonstrate that structure-aware graph modeling and spatial-frequency implicit fusion jointly improve accuracy and robustness for multimodal remote-sensing segmentation. The code will be shared on GitHub soon. Published: 2026-05-08T19:39:47+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing Score: 0.805 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Bingxia Xin; Hongqing Li; Chunyu Zhu; Rongyuan Dai; Qiong Wu

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/tgrs.2026.3691414

Relevance: 0.805 (must_read)

Multi-source remote sensing images capture complementary information of ground objects and provide valuable inputs for semantic segmentation, and different sensors may produce modality inconsistencies and interference noise. Optical and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) segmentation is challenging for three main reasons. First, geometric distortions introduce scale inconsistencies. Second, speckle noise degrades signal quality. Third, subtle inter-class differences lead to misalignment between modalities. Collectively, these factors aggravate cross-modal contextual inconsistency and hinder accurate multimodal segmentation. We propose MSFNet, a graph-guided spatial-frequency implicit fusion network that unifies graph priors with continuous implicit representations. A dual-path encoder processes optical images with a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and SAR with a Dynamic Adaptive Graph Convolutional Network(DA-GCN) to capture topological/scattering structures. Fusion is performed by Graph-guided Spatial Implicit Sampling (GSIS) for geometry-aware alignment in the continuous coordinate space and Graph-guided Fourier Implicit Sampling(GFIS) for frequency-aware detail compensation and global context modulation. A multi-scale decoder yields fine-grained boundaries. On WHU-OPT-SAR, DFC23, YYX-OPT-SAR and DFC2025, MSFNet achieves state-of-the-art performance, with consistent gains in Boundary F1-score (Boundary-F1) and boundary intersection-over-union (bIoU), as well as robustness to misregistration and speckle. These results demonstrate that structure-aware graph modeling and spatial-frequency implicit fusion jointly improve accuracy and robustness for multimodal remote-sensing segmentation. The code will be shared on GitHub soon.

item.title: DASR+: Training Domain Distance Aware Network for Unsupervised Image Super-Resolution
item.dc:creator: Xiaorui Zhao
Yunxuan Wei
Xin Deng
Yawei Li
Radu Timofte
Hengjie Song
Shuhang Gu
item.prism:publicationName: International Journal of Computer Vision
item.description: Recently, unsupervised super-resolution has attracted increasing attention due to its promising potential in real-world applications. Early studies proposed generating synthetic low-resolution (LR) images that share similar degradation characteristics with real LR images while preserving the content of high-resolution (HR) images, and utilizing the generated pseudo pairs for supervised training of super-resolution networks. Nevertheless, the SR performance of these approaches is limited by the inevitable domain gap between synthetic and real LR images. In this paper, we propose a novel domain distance aware super-resolution (DASR+) approach with newly designed domain gap aware training strategy, domain distance weighted supervision strategy, and a domain distance adaptive network for unsupervised image super-resolution. Particularly, domain gap aware training takes additional benefit from real LR images and performs feature-level as well as image-level adversarial learning to leverage real-world LR images in the target domain. In addition, domain distance weighted supervision enables a more rational use of generated LR-HR pairs using domain distance information, and the domain distance adaptive SR network injects domain distance information into the network architecture to adaptively adjust the SR mapping. As a result, the SR model trained on synthetic data can be generalized to super-resolve real-world LR images without corresponding ground-truth images. We evaluate DASR+ on both synthetic and real datasets, our approach consistently outperforms state-of-the-art unsupervised SR methods in generating results with more realistic textures. Published: 2026-05-07T19:35:06+00:00 Venue: International Journal of Computer Vision Score: 0.803 (consider)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Xiaorui Zhao; Yunxuan Wei; Xin Deng; Yawei Li; Radu Timofte; Hengjie Song; Shuhang Gu

Journal: International Journal of Computer Vision

DOI: 10.1007/s11263-026-02822-2

Relevance: 0.803 (consider)

Recently, unsupervised super-resolution has attracted increasing attention due to its promising potential in real-world applications. Early studies proposed generating synthetic low-resolution (LR) images that share similar degradation characteristics with real LR images while preserving the content of high-resolution (HR) images, and utilizing the generated pseudo pairs for supervised training of super-resolution networks. Nevertheless, the SR performance of these approaches is limited by the inevitable domain gap between synthetic and real LR images. In this paper, we propose a novel domain distance aware super-resolution (DASR+) approach with newly designed domain gap aware training strategy, domain distance weighted supervision strategy, and a domain distance adaptive network for unsupervised image super-resolution. Particularly, domain gap aware training takes additional benefit from real LR images and performs feature-level as well as image-level adversarial learning to leverage real-world LR images in the target domain. In addition, domain distance weighted supervision enables a more rational use of generated LR-HR pairs using domain distance information, and the domain distance adaptive SR network injects domain distance information into the network architecture to adaptively adjust the SR mapping. As a result, the SR model trained on synthetic data can be generalized to super-resolve real-world LR images without corresponding ground-truth images. We evaluate DASR+ on both synthetic and real datasets, our approach consistently outperforms state-of-the-art unsupervised SR methods in generating results with more realistic textures.

item.title: Cloud-Type Classification using Vision-Language Model with Cloud Multi-Modal Adapter
item.dc:creator: Shuang Liu
Yuzhu Chu
Zhong Zhang
Baihua Xiao
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
item.description: The existing methods for cloud-type classification primarily rely on cloud observation data containing low-level semantic information, e.g., cloud images and millimeter-wave cloud radar (MMCR) observations, which limits the ability to bridge the gap between original cloud data and human cognitive understanding. In this paper, we apply Large Language Model (LLM) to generate cloud description texts which include high-level semantic information, and we integrate cloud images, MMCR observations and cloud description texts for cloud-type classification. To effectively fuse the information across modalities, we propose a novel adapter named Cloud Multi-Modal Adapter (CMMA) for Vision Language Model (VLM) fine-tuning. The proposed CMMA could adaptively fuse the image features with both radar and text features extracted from corresponding adapters, which enhances the fusion across different modalities and improves the model’s ability to learn from multi-modal data. Meanwhile, we propose the Tri-Modalities Contrastive Loss (TCL) to enhance cross-modal alignment while preserving intra-modal consistency. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach. Published: 2026-05-08T19:39:47+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing Score: 0.800 (consider)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Shuang Liu; Yuzhu Chu; Zhong Zhang; Baihua Xiao

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/tgrs.2026.3691532

Relevance: 0.800 (consider)

The existing methods for cloud-type classification primarily rely on cloud observation data containing low-level semantic information, e.g., cloud images and millimeter-wave cloud radar (MMCR) observations, which limits the ability to bridge the gap between original cloud data and human cognitive understanding. In this paper, we apply Large Language Model (LLM) to generate cloud description texts which include high-level semantic information, and we integrate cloud images, MMCR observations and cloud description texts for cloud-type classification. To effectively fuse the information across modalities, we propose a novel adapter named Cloud Multi-Modal Adapter (CMMA) for Vision Language Model (VLM) fine-tuning. The proposed CMMA could adaptively fuse the image features with both radar and text features extracted from corresponding adapters, which enhances the fusion across different modalities and improves the model’s ability to learn from multi-modal data. Meanwhile, we propose the Tri-Modalities Contrastive Loss (TCL) to enhance cross-modal alignment while preserving intra-modal consistency. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach.

item.title: Prompting Rain Off: Evolving Compact Dual Prompts for Continual De-Raining
item.dc:creator: Minghao Liu
Wenhan Yang
Jiaying Liu
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
item.description: In recent years, there has been notable progress in single-image rain removal, particularly focusing on static data distributions in these approaches. When dealing with data that constantly changes, the challenge of catastrophic forgetting arises, which is quite common and critical in real-world scenarios. To address this, we propose Evolving COmpact Dual Prompt Learning (EcoDPL), an efficient rehearsal-free continual learning deraining framework designed specifically for low-level vision tasks. Specifically, we design two prompt pools at both image and feature levels and insert these prompts into images and embedding tokens, for better knowledge transfer across tasks. Our adaptive weight generation module, P-Fuser, attaches an attention map to each prompt, to adaptively pay attention to different inputs, and get different weights to fuse prompts, making the inserted prompts more flexible with various inputs. Also, we introduce Grad-Tuner, a dictionary learning strategy, to compress knowledge into fewer prompts. This makes the knowledge more compact and provides more space for new prompts to learn new tasks. Our method stands out by leveraging small, learnable prompts for efficient knowledge retention across tasks, not increasing training time or parameters. Furthermore, we present an augmented method that upgrades the distance function γ from simple cosine distance to a more advanced weight generation network. We also employ a fine-tuned dictionary learning technique, compressing knowledge into a more compact form, and enhancing the ability of prompts to learn new tasks. With our new designs, the model becomes more flexible with various inputs and it compresses knowledge into fewer prompts to free up spaces to learn new tasks. Through extensive experiments on various rain removal datasets, our EcoDPL method consistently outperforms previous continual learning techniques. Notably, although EcoDPL is designed for continual learning with changing data, it also perform... Our method stands out by leveraging small, learnable prompts for efficient knowledge retention across tasks, not increasing training time or parameters. item.title: From Weak to Strong: Cross-level Energy Field Aggregation for Infrared Small Target Detection
item.dc:creator: Jinfeng Fang
Zhixin Ma
Zhenwei Zhang
Guanglei Song
Haiting Tan
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
item.description: Infrared small target detection (IRSTD) remains a challenging task due to the lack of discriminative texture cues in targets and the presence of highly complex backgrounds. Inspired by the spectral energy distribution characteristics inherent to infrared imagery, this paper develops a novel Cross-layer Energy Field Aggregation Network (CEFANet), which focuses on target energy discontinuities embedded in cluttered backgrounds to achieve dynamic discrimination and feature enhancement of small targets with varying states. Specifically, to effectively exploit the fact that infrared targets typically manifest as high-energy responses at local scales, we introduce an Energy Field Attention (EFA) module that selectively amplifies target energy mutations while suppressing background noise. In addition, we propose a Cross-layer State Fusion (CSF) module based on channel-wise state awareness and adaptive weighting mechanisms, enabling precise modulation of low-level features under the guidance of high-level semantic states. Extensive experiments conducted on multiple public benchmarks demonstrate that the proposed CEFANet significantly improves the detection performance of infrared small targets. Published: 2026-05-07T19:56:24+00:00 Venue: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters Score: 0.799 (consider)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Jinfeng Fang; Zhixin Ma; Zhenwei Zhang; Guanglei Song; Haiting Tan

Journal: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters

DOI: 10.1109/lgrs.2026.3691152

Relevance: 0.799 (consider)

Infrared small target detection (IRSTD) remains a challenging task due to the lack of discriminative texture cues in targets and the presence of highly complex backgrounds. Inspired by the spectral energy distribution characteristics inherent to infrared imagery, this paper develops a novel Cross-layer Energy Field Aggregation Network (CEFANet), which focuses on target energy discontinuities embedded in cluttered backgrounds to achieve dynamic discrimination and feature enhancement of small targets with varying states. Specifically, to effectively exploit the fact that infrared targets typically manifest as high-energy responses at local scales, we introduce an Energy Field Attention (EFA) module that selectively amplifies target energy mutations while suppressing background noise. In addition, we propose a Cross-layer State Fusion (CSF) module based on channel-wise state awareness and adaptive weighting mechanisms, enabling precise modulation of low-level features under the guidance of high-level semantic states. Extensive experiments conducted on multiple public benchmarks demonstrate that the proposed CEFANet significantly improves the detection performance of infrared small targets.

item.title: Scalable Decomposition Gradients for Multimodal Learning
item.dc:creator: Mengxuan Wang
Hongjie Jiang
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
item.description: Optimizing multimodal models using unimodal objectives has proven effective in mitigating modality bias and enhancing generalization. This multitask-style training paradigm has motivated a range of gradient-based optimization methods. However, existing approaches predominantly focus on adjusting gradient magnitudes during aggregation, while overlooking the directional inconsistency between the final update direction and the gradients derived from unimodal and multimodal objectives, which may result in suboptimal optimization. To address this issue, we propose Scalable Decomposition Gradients (SDG), a novel framework for conflict-free optimization in multimodal learning. SDG explicitly decouples the optimization of gradient magnitude and direction. Specifically, we employ modality-aware orthogonal projections to remove conflicting components across gradients, ensuring that the final update direction aligns with both unimodal and multimodal learning objectives. We further utilize the projection residuals to adaptively scale each gradient, balancing their relative contributions and mitigating magnitude disparities. Theoretical analysis demonstrates that such conflict-free updates with consistent step sizes promote balanced convergence across losses from different modalities. Extensive experiments on several widely used multimodal benchmarks validate the effectiveness of SDG, showing notable performance improvements without introducing additional model complexity. Published: 2026-05-08T19:42:07+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology Score: 0.798 (consider)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Mengxuan Wang; Hongjie Jiang

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology

DOI: 10.1109/tcsvt.2026.3691409

Relevance: 0.798 (consider)

Optimizing multimodal models using unimodal objectives has proven effective in mitigating modality bias and enhancing generalization. This multitask-style training paradigm has motivated a range of gradient-based optimization methods. However, existing approaches predominantly focus on adjusting gradient magnitudes during aggregation, while overlooking the directional inconsistency between the final update direction and the gradients derived from unimodal and multimodal objectives, which may result in suboptimal optimization. To address this issue, we propose Scalable Decomposition Gradients (SDG), a novel framework for conflict-free optimization in multimodal learning. SDG explicitly decouples the optimization of gradient magnitude and direction. Specifically, we employ modality-aware orthogonal projections to remove conflicting components across gradients, ensuring that the final update direction aligns with both unimodal and multimodal learning objectives. We further utilize the projection residuals to adaptively scale each gradient, balancing their relative contributions and mitigating magnitude disparities. Theoretical analysis demonstrates that such conflict-free updates with consistent step sizes promote balanced convergence across losses from different modalities. Extensive experiments on several widely used multimodal benchmarks validate the effectiveness of SDG, showing notable performance improvements without introducing additional model complexity.

item.title: R2C-MGR2L: Enhancing Multi-granularity Relational Representation in Dual-temporal Optical Remote Sensing Images through LLM-based Scene Graph Generation
item.dc:creator: Lilu Zhu
Xiaolu Su
Jiaxuan Tang
Yang Wang
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
item.description: Recent advances in Earth observations have enabled the acquisition of high-precision optical remote sensing imagery (ORSI) with sub-meter spatial resolution and hourly revisit periods. While early neural network-based methods have demonstrated notable limitations in visual perception, more recent multimodal fusion approaches still struggle to effectively recognize and reason over geospatial object relations. To fully promote the collaborative analysis of very-high-resolution (VHR) dual-temporal ORSIs, this paper presents a novel framework named R2C-MGR2L that is designed to enhance multi-granularity relational representation in dual-temporal optical remote sensing images through LLM-based scene graph generation (SGG). This framework presents a two-stage learning paradigm—knowledge alignment and cross-modal fusion—that incorporates structured knowledge representations and a hierarchical attention mechanism to improve the refinement and reasoning of fine-grained relational semantics. First, we investigate the types of geospatial object relations and assess the feasibility of enhancing relational semantic representation through multimodal knowledge extraction and embedding. We subsequently employ a pre-trained code large language model (Code-LLM) to transform multigranularity relation triplets from extracted optical remote sensing (ORS) scene graphs into semantically equivalent programs, thereby alleviating the scarcity of domain-specific ORSI knowledge. Moreover, a hybrid-structured asymmetric contrastive learning network is developed to further refine the semantic representations derived from complex scenes. By hierarchically fusing dual-temporal ORSIs and corresponding scene graphs, an integrated representation of visual targets and structural knowledge across multiple depths is achieved. We evaluate our proposed framework on three relation-aware ORS scene understanding tasks. The experimental results suggest that the R2C-MGR2L outperforms the state-of-the-art gener... item.title: Towards the Connection between Activation Sparsity and Flat Minima
item.dc:creator: Ze Peng
Jian Zhang
Lei Qi
Yang Gao
Yinghuan Shi
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
item.description: The observation that activation sparsity emerges in MLP blocks of standardly trained Transformers offers an opportunity to drastically reduce computation costs without sacrificing performance. To theoretically explain this phenomenon, existing works have shown that activation sparsity does not result from the data properties or data fitting but from the implicit bias of the training process. However, these connections are obtained with strong assumptions (e.g., shallow networks, a small number of training steps, and special training techniques), which cannot be applied to deep models standardly trained with a large number of steps. Different from these works, we find that the flatness of loss landscapes is also closely related to the MLP activation sparsity and can serve as a weaker assumption because it naturally emerges in the standard training of deep networks without the above strong assumptions. Specifically, we find that 1) the MLP activation sparsity equals a ratio between “augmented f latness” (a weighted sum of flatness measures) and the product of the input norm and activation gradient of the MLP. We empirically find that this ratio decreases during training, leading to sparse activations. 2) We also propose the notion of derivative sparsity, which reduces to activation sparsity under ReLU, but further enables pruning in the backward propagation and is more stable than activation sparsity. With the theoretical findings, we can further encourage activation sparsity by decreasing the numerator and increasing the denominator of the ratio: 1) To improve (lower) the flatness, we add different bias vectors to input tokens of MLP blocks to strengthen stochastic gradient noises that drive the model to a flat area. 2) We restrict the lower bound of affine parameters in LayerNorm to increase the input norm of MLPs. 3) To increase the activation sparsity, we propose an activation function JSReLU to encourage the search of parameters with sparse derivatives and sparse activations. These plug-and-play modifications can effectively reduce the ratio and produce sparser activations. Experiments on ImageNet-1K and C4 demonstrate relative improvements of at least 36% on inference sparsity and at least 50% on training sparsity over vanilla Transformers, indicating further potential cost reduction in both inference and training. Published: 2026-05-07T19:53:00+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence Score: 0.796 (consider)
